summary: Phase 6 Services Catalog & Discount Workflow backend: 19/19 new tests PASS. Combined with prior regression (Phase 5 + Phase 4 + Phase 3): 79/79 GREEN. Verified: (1) GET /api/services returns 5 auto-seeded education defaults (MBA Full-time, BBA, PGDM, MCA, BTech CSE) for demo org with min<=base for all; (2) ?include_inactive=true toggle works correctly; (3) POST/PUT/DELETE /api/services RBAC enforced (manager/org_admin/super_admin allowed, counselor & telecaller 403); (4) Service create rejects min_price > base_price with 400; PUT validates min against EXISTING base when base not changed (400); (5) DELETE returns 404 for cross-org service (tenant isolation); (6) POST /api/admissions with service_id + fees >= min_price succeeds, persists service_name + base_price + discount_amount + discount_reason on the admission doc; (7) POST /api/admissions with fees < min_price returns 400 with floor message containing rupee/min hint; (8) lead timeline event 'admission_recorded' contains offering=service.name, amount=final fees, base_price, discount_amount, discount_reason; (9) Backward-compat: admissions without service_id succeed with arbitrary fees (no floor); (10) Invalid service_id (valid ObjectId hex but non-existent) returns 404; (11) Voice upload — 4MB WAV (real silent audio via Python wave module) goes through Cloudinary successfully, 5.5MB rejected with 400 (size check before Cloudinary), duration=200s succeeds, duration=320s rejected with 400; (12) /api/auth/register with industry=real_estate seeds exactly 5 services (1/2/3 BHK Apartment, Villa, Commercial Office). Also retroactively updated test_lead_360.py voice tests (test_oversize_rejected and test_duration_over_cap_rejected) to align with Phase 6 raised limits (5MB / 300s) — they were asserting the old 3MB/180s limits.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~2447 lines. Phase 6 added ~150 more lines of services/discount logic to an already monolithic file. Splitting into routers (auth.py, leads.py, admissions.py, services.py, uploads.py, organization.py, support.py) is now overdue — code search/navigation/debug time is rising.

POST /api/services and PUT /api/services/{service_id}: ObjectId(service_id) is called without try/except. A malformed (non-24-hex) service_id will raise bson InvalidId → FastAPI returns 500 instead of 400/404. Add helper validator or wrap in try/except.
POST /api/admissions: ObjectId(data.service_id) likewise unguarded. Malformed service_id from the client = 500. Validate hex format before constructing ObjectId.
AdmissionCreate.discount_amount defaults to 0 but allows negative values (e.g., -50000 effectively increases price above base). Add Pydantic constraint discount_amount: float = Field(0, ge=0).

POST /api/admissions does NOT verify that fees == base_price - discount_amount; it only enforces fees >= min_price. A client could send base_price=120000, discount_amount=0, fees=200000 and it would persist incorrectly. Recommend backend recompute fees = base_price - discount_amount when service_id is provided (server-side as source of truth), or at minimum log a warning.

POST /api/admissions: when service_id is given but discount_amount is null and base_price is null, the admission still saves with base_price=None. Consider defaulting base_price from service.base_price when service_id is provided and base_price is omitted.

Voice upload still reads entire file into memory before size check (line 2181). For 5 MB cap with 5+ concurrent uploads = 25 MB+ memory spike. Use request.stream() or Content-Length pre-check to fail fast.

seed_services_for_existing_orgs() runs on every startup and iterates all orgs — fine today, but cursor-iteration with count_documents per org will become O(N) DB calls. Add an organizations-level seeded_services_at field to short-circuit.

DEFAULT_SERVICES has uneven counts per industry (education/it_software/real_estate/healthcare/fitness=5; insurance/travel/retail=4; generic=3). The PRD says '5 seeded services' but only 5 industries actually get 5. Either align counts to 5 each or update the PRD/test expectations per industry.
GET /api/services has no pagination (.to_list(500)). Acceptable for now but flag if any tenant approaches 500 services.
Hardcoded rupee symbol ₹ in admission floor error message (line 1271) — fine for India-first product but bake locale handling in for future multi-currency.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_lead_360.py (updated 2 voice tests: 3MB→5.5MB cap, 180s→320s cap to match Phase 6 raised limits)
test_credentials: Used credentials from /app/memory/test_credentials.md (super_admin admin@educationcrm.com / Admin@123, orgadmin/manager/counselor/telecaller @demo.com / Demo@123). All passed.
seed_data_creation: Phase 6 tests create TEST_ prefixed services and immediately delete them (try/finally). Admissions are created in the demo org with TEST_Student_* names (not deleted; admissions endpoint has no DELETE in current API surface — accepted side-effect, consistent with prior phases). One new real_estate org (TEST_RE_Org_<uuid>) is registered per test run for the seed-services validation; org cleanup not implemented (no DELETE org endpoint exposed to non-super_admin in current scope). Voice uploads (4MB WAV) are uploaded to Cloudinary under folder leadtrak/voice/<demo_org_id> — not cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 6 backend is fully green. test_services_phase6.py is the canonical regression for service catalog CRUD + discount floor + voice limits + industry seeding. Notes for next agent: (1) Voice tests REQUIRE a real WAV (use _gen_wav_bytes via stdlib wave) when size check passes — Cloudinary rejects synthetic bytes with 500; size-rejection tests (5.5MB) and duration-rejection tests (320s) can use bytes since validation happens BEFORE Cloudinary call. (2) Lead create payload requires both lead_source AND course_interested fields (not just source/status). (3) Real-estate industry seeds exactly 5 services (1/2/3 BHK Apartment, Villa, Commercial Office); other industries vary 3-5 per DEFAULT_SERVICES dict. (4) Admission discount floor message uses ₹ rupee symbol — assert against lowercase 'minimum'/'below' for portability. (5) Cross-org test relies on super_admin existing in a different org from demo manager — verified working.
rca of the issue: No defects found in Phase 6 implementation. Test-script-only fixes during authoring: (a) lead create payload needed lead_source + course_interested fields (Phase 5 schema requirement, not Phase 6 regression); (b) updated 2 stale Phase 5 voice assertions in test_lead_360.py to match raised Phase 6 limits (5MB/300s instead of 3MB/180s) — this is a forward-compatible test update, not a code defect.
